title: Default Assignment Files
text: Default Assignment Files Default assignment files for Uniface products and servers are automatically created when you install Uniface. Assignment files that are common to several Uniface products and servers are located in the /common/adm subdirectory of the installation. Assignment files that are specific to a Uniface product are located in the product's /adm subdirectory. The following assignment files are used by Uniface: Table 1. Default Assignment Files Assignment File Default Location Description dbms.asn /common Configures the database connector, logon path, and options usys.asn /common Used by all Uniface applications. urouter.asn /common Used by the Uniface Router urmon.asn /common Used by the Uniface Router Monitor ide.asn /uniface Used by the Uniface IDE userver.asn /uniface Used the Uniface Server wasv.asn /uniface Used by the Uniface Web Application Server usyschr.asn /uniface Used by all Uniface applications running in character mode. Not created on iSeries. On iSeries, assignment files are created in the installation library, as separate members in file ASN . Related Links dbms.asn The dbms.asn file is a basic assignment file containing the DBMS assignment settings. usys.asn Every Uniface application looks for global assignments in a file called usys.asn in the USYSADM: (/adm) subdirectory of the Uniface installation directory. urouter.asn Assignment file for the Uniface Router. You can use the urouter.asn file to specify settings, paths to connectors, and Uniface Server environment definitions, such as USTs. urmon.asn Assignment file for the Uniface Router Monitor. You can use the urmon.asn file to specify settings that relate to the Uniface Router Monitor. wasv.asn The wasv.asn file is a basic assignment file for the Web Application Server. usyschr.asn The usyschr.asn file contains default widget settings for the character mode interface.
